example: A good talk can electrify an audience and even change the world.
ja: 興奮させる、感電させる

example: They bring people together, spark curiosity, and inspire action.
ja: 火花を散らす、引き起こす

example: The right idea can ripple across the planet at the speed of light.
Your number one mission as a speaker is to take something that matters deeply to you.
If you can conjure up an exciting idea in someone's mind, you have done something wondrous.
ja: （魔法のように）呼び起こす、思い浮かべる

example: She was showing the audience just how weird a phenomenon laughter is.
And in fact, how primitive laughter is as a sound.
Laughter exists as a way human beings form bonds with one another.
Her research shows that laughing strengthens relationships.
It's a biological process through which we can connect with one another.
A speaker has to deliver the idea in a way that the audience can understand.
You, the speaker, are the trusty tour guide.
ja: 信頼できる、頼りになる

example: Must be careful not to lose anyone by rushing ahead or constantly changing direction.
A giant orange parrot dancing on the elephant's head and shrieking over and over.
The fact that we can transfer ideas in this way is why speaking skills are so important.
Speakers who have figured out how to spread their ideas have the power to make an incredible impact.
Do you have ideas that deserve a wider audience?

ja: 明らかにする、発見する
